Call an ordered triple $(a, b, c)$ of integers feral if $b - a$, $c - a$, and $c - b$ are all prime numbers. Find the number of feral triples where $1 \leq a < b < c \leq 20$.

Remember to put your answer on its own line after "Answer:".
label_hint:
Consider the three differences \(b-a\), \(c-b\), and \(c-a\); they must all be prime.  
Since \(1\le a<b<c\le20\), all differences are at most 19, so list the primes \(\le19\).  
Pick a value for \(a\) and then choose two distinct primes \(p\) and \(q\) for \(b-a\) and \(c-b\); finally check whether \(p+q\) (which equals \(c-a\)) is also prime.  
Count the resulting triples.
